original_image_tags: A scanned page of a typed carbon copy of a memorandum, likely on thin paper or onion skin, showing bleed-through from text on the reverse side. The paper is aged and creased. There are staple holes or punch holes at the top with some discoloration around them. The text is mirrored/bleed-through at the top and bottom of the page. The main body text is oriented normally. There are multiple stamps, including a "RECEIVED" stamp at the bottom right dated AUG 17 9:30 AM '48, and a red stamp on the left side, partially obscured.

elements.description: A typed letter on light-colored paper, oriented upside down relative to the viewer. The document is a carbon copy of a letter from Edwin T. Holmes (Chief, Division of Export Control) to Director, FBI, dated August 10, 1948, regarding Mrs. Madeline Conway Merckens and international commerce. It includes typed text, a handwritten signature, and a 'RECEIVED' stamp from the FBI Internal Security Division dated AUG 17 9:30 AM. There are two hole-punch reinforcements at the top edge.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the presence of a 'RECEIVED' stamp with the same date and time, but they differ on the stamp's location and the description of the paper's physical orientation, though these are not direct contradictions on specific quoted text.
